macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed Beige Book shows slight-to-moderate growth, mixed consumer spending, and higher inflation pressures
items.summary: The Federal Reserve’s latest Beige Book said U.S. economic activity rose at a slight-to-moderate pace in most districts, while consumer spending stayed mixed and more bifurcated by income level. It also flagged rising delinquencies in some lending categories and noted that inflation picked up, with energy-related costs linked to Middle East tensions pressuring shipping, groceries, fertilizer, and margins.

items.title: Wall Street weakens as renewed U.S.-Iran tensions and higher oil prices hit risk assets
items.summary: Midday market coverage reported the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 hitting session lows as renewed tensions between the U.S. and Iran clouded the outlook for a broader peace deal. Brent crude moved toward $98 a barrel and WTI around $96, adding pressure to equities and lifting energy-market volatility.

items.title: Private credit stocks fall on rising redemption requests at Cliffwater fund
items.summary: Shares tied to major alternative-asset managers including Blackstone, KKR, and Apollo were lower after Cliffwater’s flagship private credit fund reported a jump in redemption requests. The move revived investor concerns about stress in the private credit industry and broader liquidity conditions.

items.title: Macro backdrop shifts as investors reassess growth, inflation, and central bank stance
items.summary: Recent market commentary said the S&P 500’s win streak ended as a hotter-than-expected economy, more hawkish central banks, and falling oil inventories pushed markets to refocus on macro risks. The note argued that the tape is increasingly being driven by growth and inflation concerns rather than just optimism around equities.

items.title: AI-driven capital spending remains a key support for markets despite signs of strain in household finances
items.summary: PIMCO said AI-driven capital expenditure continues to support investment, equity markets, and the wealth effect for higher-income households, helping keep the U.S. economy resilient. At the same time, it warned that tariffs, higher energy prices, and slower wage growth have sharply reduced real disposable personal income, indicating broader consumer strain beneath headline growth.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin drops toward a 4-month low amid heavy liquidations
items.summary: Bitcoin fell to around $61,500 and over $1 billion in leveraged crypto positions were liquidated in 24 hours, marking one of the sharpest recent market selloffs. Analysts cited ETF outflows and long-term holder selling as contributing factors.

items.title: Crypto market rout wipes out billions as Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP slide
items.summary: The total cryptocurrency market cap fell sharply, with Bitcoin dropping below $67,000, Ethereum near $1,870, and XRP near $1.23. The report said about $1.50 billion in long positions and $233 million in short positions were liquidated in a single day.

items.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs extend outflow streak as sentiment weakens
items.summary: Crypto market coverage cited 12 consecutive days of outflows from Bitcoin spot ETFs, adding pressure to prices. The outflows were presented as a key factor behind deteriorating market sentiment.

items.title: Treasury sanctions major Iranian cryptocurrency exchange
items.summary: U.S. authorities imposed sanctions on a major Iranian cryptocurrency exchange, adding to regulatory pressure in the sector. The move was highlighted alongside broader U.S. actions targeting Iran-linked crypto activity.

items.title: Mastercard expands stablecoin settlement support
items.summary: Mastercard announced broader support for stablecoin transactions and settlement, including partnerships tied to Ripple’s RLUSD and Circle’s USDC. The development signals continued integration of crypto into mainstream payments infrastructure.

items.title: Stripe, Visa, and Mastercard-linked stablecoin platform advances
items.summary: Industry coverage said major payments firms are backing a new stablecoin platform, underscoring strong project momentum in payment-focused blockchain infrastructure. The news was framed as one of the few bright spots in a weak crypto market.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Microsoft reportedly unveils seven new MAI models at Build, including a reasoning model, coding model, image model and transcription model
items.summary: Coverage from a June 3 daily AI news roundup says Microsoft announced seven new MAI models at Build, positioning them as cheaper and competitive on benchmarks, alongside a new Frontier Tuning tool and a Mayo Clinic collaboration on healthcare AI. The roundup also says Microsoft emphasized the models were not trained on copyrighted data or the entire internet.
items.source: Daily AI News from GAI Insights (YouTube)

items.title: Google AI tracker highlights major infrastructure and product moves, including TPU roadmap changes and Gemini Enterprise expansion
items.summary: An AI Weekly tracker says Google split its accelerator roadmap into TPU 8t for training and TPU 8i for inference, with the inference chip claimed to deliver better perf-per-dollar for MoE serving. It also cites the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, new native plug-ins, and a reported confirmation that the next Apple Siri will be powered by Gemini.

items.title: MIT researchers publish new work on teaching AI agents to ask better questions in collaborative games
items.summary: MIT News reports a new study where AI models playing Collaborative Battleship struggled to ask informative questions about hidden ships, and a Monte Carlo inference strategy improved their question-asking behavior. The work adds to recent research on better agent planning and information-seeking in multi-agent settings.

items.title: Recent machine learning research papers appear on arXiv across theory, optimization and diffusion models
items.summary: The latest arXiv Machine Learning listing includes new submissions on chain-of-thought theory in in-context learning, knockoffs-based false discovery rate control for deep neural networks, and diffusion models for incomplete tabular data. These posts reflect active research in reasoning, statistical guarantees and robust model training.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
